varieties.name: Jacaranda mimosifolia 'Standard Purple'
varieties.origin: Argentina/Brazil, introduced to Brisbane via England 1850s
varieties.traits: Fast-growing deciduous tree, 8-15m height, fern-like bipinnate foliage, vase-shaped crown to 10-12m spread
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific flowering once mature (7-10 years from seed, 2-3 years if grafted)
varieties.availability: Delivertree, Ross Evans Garden Centre, All Green Nursery (Melbourne), Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Speciality Trees (Melbourne to QLD delivery), Plantmark, Online Plants Australia, True Green Nursery

varieties.name: Jacaranda mimosifolia 'Alba' (White Christmas)
varieties.origin: Cultivar selection from J. mimosifolia, developed for ornamental horticulture
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree 10-15m height, similar growth habit to purple form, fern-like foliage, spreading canopy 10-12m

varieties.name: Jacaranda mimosifolia 'Bonsai Blue'
varieties.origin: Modern dwarf cultivar bred for compact growth
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety 3-4m height, compact fern-like foliage, manageable canopy 2-3m spread
varieties.yield_notes: Good flowering for compact size, better indoor blooming than standard
varieties.availability: Ross Evans Garden Centre, specialty nurseries, garden centers (limited stock)

varieties.name: Jacaranda cuspidifolia
varieties.origin: Bolivia to Brazil and Argentina (Salta), native to seasonally dry tropical regions
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree 4-8m (up to 20m in nature), more compact inflorescences than J. mimosifolia, pointed bipinnate leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Very dependable heavy bloomer annually, more reliable than J. mimosifolia
varieties.availability: Gardino Nursery (Florida, ships to Australia), specialty collectors, harder to find than J. mimosifolia

varieties.name: Jacaranda obtusifolia
varieties.origin: South Tropical America (Trinidad, Tobago, Venezuela), wet tropical biome
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree/large shrub 6-11m height, broader less-pinnate leaves than other Jacarandas, denser foliage, rounded canopy
varieties.availability: Rare in cultivation, botanical gardens, specialty nurseries only

varieties.name: Jacaranda copaia
varieties.origin: Central & South Tropical America (Amazon Basin), wet tropical biome
varieties.traits: Tall tree 15-30m+ height, straight cylindrical trunk, large bipinnate leaves, narrow crown
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate flowering, valued more for timber than ornamental flowers
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical nurseries, botanical institutions, forestry suppliers

varieties.name: Historical Brisbane/Queensland Plantings
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane received mature Jacaranda plants, seedlings and seeds from around the world starting 1855. Major street plantings occurred 1930s-1970s during beautification programs. Trees were offered free to residents in 1970s with conditions. Some original 1914 New Farm Park trees have fallen due to age (100+ years). Goodna has some of Southeast Queensland's best Jacaranda displays from 1932 plantings

varieties.name: Jacaranda mimosifolia (Neeli Gulmohar/Neel Mohar)
varieties.origin: South America, introduced to India via colonial period, widely planted in Kerala
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree 8-15m height in Kerala conditions, fern-like pinnate leaves, rounded crown 10-12m spread
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific flowering, spectacular display during blooming season
varieties.availability: Nurserylive (all India delivery), Plantsguru, Exotic Flora (Kadiyam nursery, all India), Garden World, Greenverz, local Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Jacaranda mimosifolia 'Alba' (White Jacaranda)
varieties.traits: Similar growth to purple form, 8-12m height in tropical conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Sparser blooms than purple but extended season
varieties.availability: Very limited in India, specialty orders only, Amazon India (seeds occasionally)

varieties.name: Jacaranda copaia (Caroba, Para-para)
varieties.traits: Tall fast-growing tree 20-35m, straight trunk, pioneer species
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate flowering, valued for timber and rapid growth
varieties.availability: Forestry nurseries, agroforestry suppliers, specialty tropical plant sources

varieties.name: Jacaranda caroba
varieties.traits: Smaller tree/shrub 4-10m, leathery dark green leaves, adapted to dry savanna but tolerant
varieties.availability: Rare in India, ethnobotanical/medicinal plant nurseries, botanical gardens

varieties.name: Jacaranda jasminoides (Maroon Jacaranda)
varieties.availability: Specialty ornamental nurseries, limited availability in India

varieties.name: Jacaranda jasminoides 'Maroon'
varieties.availability: Rare cultivar, specialty collectors, ornamental plant nurseries

varieties.name: Jacaranda caucana
varieties.traits: Medium tree 6-12m, rounded canopy, attractive foliage
varieties.availability: Very rare in India, botanical institutions, specialty tropical collectors

varieties.name: Jacaranda cuspidifolia
varieties.origin: Bolivia, Brazil, Argentina - seasonally dry tropical
varieties.availability: Rare in India, specialty imports, botanical collections

varieties.name: Colonial Era Munnar Plantings
varieties.growing_notes: Jacaranda and Spathodea trees planted by British officials near estate bungalows at same time. Spathodea controlled mosquitoes for malaria prevention. Jacaranda bloom (February-April) indicates spring season in hill stations. Trees planted atop hills and along roadsides for beautification. Create 'river of purple' effect during peak bloom with tea plantation green background
